topic:
Gemini said
To get the best results for your Facebook campaign, you need to use keywords that resonate with the Israeli educational system (Gefen/Ministry of Education) and the "Startup Nation" culture that parents value.

Here are the search terms and keywords categorized by your target audience:

1. Professional & Educational (For Principals/Schools)
These terms are crucial if you are trying to get the program into schools through official channels like the Gefen (גפ"ן) system.

תוכנית גפ"ן (Gefen Program - the official Ministry of Education catalog)

למידה חווייתית (Experiential Learning)

מיומנויות המאה ה-21 (21st Century Skills)

חינוך ליזמות (Entrepreneurship Education)

כישורי חיים (SEL) (Life Skills / Social-Emotional Learning)

חדשנות בחינוך (Innovation in Education)

סדנאות העשרה לבתי ספר (Enrichment workshops for schools)

2. Parental & Aspirational (For Parents/Teens)
These terms trigger the desire for parents to give their children a head start in life and the military (especially units like 8200).

יזמים צעירים (Young Entrepreneurs)

חינוך פיננסי לנוער (Financial Education for Youth)

פיתוח מנהיגות (Leadership Development)

חוג יזמות (Entrepreneurship Club/After-school activity)

עמידה מול קהל (Public Speaking)

ביטחון עצמי לנוער (Self-confidence for youth)

הקמת סטארטאפ (Starting a Startup)

3. Business & Technical (For Meta Ad Targeting)
When setting up your Facebook "Detailed Targeting," use these Hebrew/English interests:

ניהול פיננסי (Financial Management)

שיווק ומכירות (Marketing and Sales)

חשיבה יצירתית (Creative Thinking)

Startup Nation

High-tech (הייטק)

Parenting teenagers (הורות למתבגרים)

keyInsights.insight: Time constraints are primary barrier to technology adoption
Training and support are critical for successful adoption
Students report improved engagement with digital tools

statistics.stat: 78% of Israeli teachers lack time for professional development
Only 42% of teachers feel confident with edtech tools
65% of students engaged better with hybrid learning
EdTech market growing 15% YoY in Israel
